lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] We're {{ELAPSED}} in now. The night is settling.
I want you to find a place where you can sit. Doesn't have to be comfortable. Just safe.
Have you noticed the moon tonight? [pause] Full. Bright. You can see the whole street if you look.
But you're not looking. You're staying where you are. That's good. That's the choice.
Let's begin with the breath. In through the nose. [pause] Four counts. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold it. [pause] The air is yours right now. Nothing else is. Just the air.
Release. [pause] Slowly. There's no rush.
Again. In. [pause] Four counts. You're doing this because you chose to.
Not because you were forced. Not because there was nowhere else to go. You chose.
That matters. The Order teaches that choice is the only real power we have left.
Your body right now. Scan it. Start at your toes. Are they cold? [pause] Let them be cold.
Your shins. Your knees. Any tension there? [pause] Where does tension come from?
It comes from holding. From clenching. From believing you have to protect something.
You don't. [pause] Not tonight. Your door is locked. Your family is inside. You've done everything you can.
Now move the scan upward. Thighs. Hips. Is there pain there you've been ignoring all day?
Name it. Don't fix it. Just see it. [pause] That's acceptance.
Your stomach. That's where we keep it. The fear. The guilt. The things we didn't say.
There's someone you're not forgiving. You know who. [pause] You don't have to forgive them tonight.
You just have to acknowledge that you're carrying them. [pause] Let them get a little lighter.
Your chest. Your heart. It's working so hard. It's been working all night.
Thank it. [pause] Really. Your heart doesn't know this is the Purge. It just knows it has to keep you alive.
Your shoulders. Where the weight lives. [pause] Drop them. Just let them fall.
Your neck. Your jaw. People clench here. Are you? [pause] You can stop.
Your face. The mask you wear. [pause] Tonight it can rest.
Your head. The place where all the words live. [pause] ka-sha-ma-tehva-no.
Breathe. [pause] You're safe. The words can settle.
I want to tell you something. [pause] The Sisters have a saying.
We say: The ones outside are not your responsibility.
The ones outside made their choices. You made yours.
The light from the moon — have you noticed how it reaches through the windows? [pause] It doesn't ask permission.
It just arrives. [pause] Like consequences.
But the light is also neutral. [pause] It illuminates. It doesn't judge.
So you can be illuminated too. [pause] Right here. Right now. With all your choices visible.
And still be safe.
I'm going to ask you to do something. [pause] Visualize your front door.
See it exactly as it is. [pause] The color. The hardware. Any scratches.
Now I want you to imagine something standing on the other side.
Not a person. [pause] Something. You know what it is.
Don't be afraid. [pause] Just see it there.
And now see yourself choosing not to open the door.
[pause] That's your power. That's the only power. The power to choose.
The ones outside — they made their choices too. [pause] Some of them are making them right now.
You don't have to witness that. You don't have to understand it.
You just have to stay. [pause] In this room. With this breath.
The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. [pause] We have a long night still.
But you will make it. Because you already have. Every minute you haven't opened the door is a choice.
Every breath you've taken is proof.
Let's breathe together one more time. [pause] In. Four counts.
Hold. [pause] The silence is yours.
Release. [pause] And rest in the knowing that you are exactly where you need to be.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide kills: 61 cumulative (hour 4 ending). Up 23 from last hour. East Side remains hottest zone.
Capitol Square: perimeter secure. Downtown: cleanup crews staged for post-dawn. State Street: coordinated group activity reported 23:18–23:47.
Yahara River bridge (Willy Street): neighborhood watch holding defensive position. Zero breach attempts last 47 minutes.
"The light doesn't ask permission. Neither do you."
"Carrying them is not the same as forgiving them."
PurgeShield Panels: 4,200 installed Madison-wide last year. Still time to upgrade. Call 1-800-SHIELDS-1.
East Side residents: reports of building-access breaches near Schenk's Corners. Secure upper floors. Do not respond to knocks.
